title: veronica polita subsp. polita
intro: Veronica polita subsp. polita, also known as the small or common speedwell, is a small, annual or biennial plant with blue, four-petaled flowers that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Veronica polita subsp. polita include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and reduce fever. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
